summary: PR #2073 matched and improved multiple video intro/cutscene functions across src/melee/vi, with supporting header/signature updates in gr, pl, mn, item, and fighter-related files. Bot evidence reported 15 new matches and 23 improvements, while later author text noted broken/regressed matches would be fixed. Review comments flagged two vi functions where raw byte/offset-style access should be pulled into a struct, preferably, or represented with M2C_FIELD.

key_files.role: major vi cutscene matching work; added un_8031FD18_OnEnter and related functions, with review feedback requesting a struct or M2C_FIELD for raw input/data access
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists un_8031F9D8 as new match and un_8031FD18_OnEnter/fn_8031FAA8 improvements

what_changed: Matched or improved many video intro/cutscene functions across vi0102, vi0401, vi0402, vi0501, vi0502, vi0601, vi0801, vi1101, vi1201v1, vi1201v2, and vi1202.

Bot report recorded 15 new matches, including un_8031F07C_OnEnter, un_8031EBBC_OnEnter, un_8031E9B8, un_803205F4, un_80321178, un_8031ED70, un_80320508, un_8031F9D8, un_8031EE60, fn_80320A1C, un_803224DC, un_80322258, fn_8031F56C, un_80321AF4, and un_80321BF8.

Bot report recorded 23 improvements in unmatched functions and data sections, concentrated in vi1201v1, vi0501, vi0502, vi0601, vi1202, vi0801, vi1201v2, vi0401, and related data/sdata/sdata2 sections.
Updated cross-module headers for vi callsites, including grbigblueroute.h, grcorneria.h, mncharsel.h, plbonuslib.h, and several item headers.
Synchronized pl_8003FDA0 body and header from one int parameter to two int parameters.
Moved it_804D6D40 declarations out of some item source files into item headers and changed the defining type in it_2725.c from f32** to f32*.
Replaced at least one M2C_FIELD byte-array write in vi0402.c with direct array indexing of un_804D6F84.
Changed vi0102 camera callback code from a vi_RunCamera helper call to explicit HSD_CObjSetCurrent/erase/draw/end logic.
Added or adjusted includes/static declarations needed for newly decompiled vi functions.
Author later commented that broken/regressed matches would be fixed; the loaded slice does not identify which functions were broken or the final fix details.

smart_moves.text: Header prototypes were updated alongside callsite/body evidence rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for functions used by vi code.
evidence_refs: pr_body: cross-module header updates for gr/ and pl/ signatures used by vi functions

smart_moves.text: For a simple byte buffer in vi0402.c, the PR replaced M2C_FIELD writes with direct indexed array writes, making the data access clearer.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: un_8031D9E4 changes M2C_FIELD(&un_804D6F84, u8*, 0/1/3) to un_804D6F84[0/1/3]

smart_moves.text: The PR used existing baselib and game helpers for scene construction in vi0501.c, including lbArchive_LoadSymbols, GObj_Create, HSD_FogLoadDesc, HSD_JObjLoadJoint, animation requests, and audio calls.
The author acknowledged broken/regressed matches instead of silently ignoring report problems.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: issue comment 'oops, will fix the broken/regressed matches'

decomp_lessons.text: When matching vi intro/cutscene entry points, recovered code may need coordinated header signature cleanup in other modules that the sequence calls into; keep those updates tied to callsite/body evidence.
evidence_refs: pr_body: necessary cross-module header updates for gr/ and pl/ signatures used by vi functions
diff_excerpt: grbigblueroute.h, grcorneria.h, plbonuslib.h, mncharsel.h signature changes

decomp_lessons.text: If a function reads structured input bytes or global state through raw offsets, reviewers may prefer recovering a small struct; M2C_FIELD or raw pointer arithmetic is a fallback, not the preferred endpoint.
evidence_refs: review_comments: vi1201v1.c 'pull out into a struct (preferred) or use M2C_FIELD'
review_comments: vi1202.c 'pull out into struct (preferred) or use M2C_FIELD'

decomp_lessons.text: For byte arrays, direct indexing can be a cleaner representation than M2C_FIELD when the accessed object is already known to be an array or byte buffer.

Bot match reports can show both function matches and data-section movement; a later author note about regressions should be recorded as uncertainty unless the slice identifies the final corrected code.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author issue comment about broken/regressed matches

naming_conventions.text: The PR retained address-style/generated names for still-unclear vi functions and globals, such as un_8031FD18_OnEnter, fn_8031FAA8, un_804D6FFC, and un_804D7050.
Local variable names in added vi code are role-based where clear: input, char_index, fog_gobj, light_gobj, camera_gobj, model_gobj, cobj, jobj, lobj, fog.
Header parameters were sometimes named when roles were obvious, such as vi0102_CameraCallback(HSD_GObj* gobj, int unused) and un_8031DE58_OnEnter(void* arg).

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used explicit object setup sequences for scene construction: load archive symbols, create fog/light/camera/model GObjs, attach HSD objects, set GX links, request animations, and install procs.
Adjusted local declaration scope/order to affect matching, such as hoisting light_gobj in vi0402.c and adding ptr in vi0401.c.
Used HSD_ASSERT before dereferencing a global pointer in vi0401.c and collapsed three Vec3 component assignments into a struct assignment.
Changed literal expression shape for a 64-bit audio argument from 0x10000000000ULL to (u64) 0x100 << 32.
Retained PAD_STACK in at least one touched item function; no verifier rationale for that specific tactic is included in the loaded slice.

Expanded vi0102_CameraCallback from a helper call into explicit HSD camera/erase/draw/end calls, likely for local code shape, but the slice does not include reviewer or objdiff rationale for the expansion.

notes: The slice includes strong metadata, changed-file counts, PR body, bot report, and two review comments.
The raw diff is truncated, so not every changed file's exact hunk is visible in the loaded evidence.
The bot report provides match/improvement numbers, but the author later noted broken/regressed matches; the slice does not identify the final corrected symbols or exact regression resolution.
Review comments are authored by itsgrimetime in the loaded data; the slice does not show whether they were self-review notes or externally requested changes.
No local build, objdiff command output, or final checkdiff logs are included beyond the bot report.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer structs for repeated raw vi input/state offset access
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2073, review comments on vi1201v1.c and vi1202.c explicitly requested pulling raw offset/input accesses into a struct where possible, with M2C_FIELD as a fallback. This supports the existing typed-fields-over-pointer-math standard for vi sequence work.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Coordinate vi function matching with real cross-module prototype cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR body says vi sequence matching required cross-module gr/ and pl/ header updates, and the diff shows UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or incomplete prototypes replaced with typed signatures. This is a PR-specific example of updating owning headers when bodies or callsites prove signatures.

source_update_candidates.title: vi1201v1 raw input/state access should be modeled structurally where possible
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2073 review feedback on src/melee/vi/vi1201v1.c requested pulling the shown raw input/state access pattern into a struct, preferred, or using M2C_FIELD as a fallback.
source_update_candidates.reason: May help future vi1201v1 cleanup avoid raw byte/pointer arithmetic when a local struct can describe the input/state layout.

source_update_candidates.title: vi1202 un_804D7050/mpLib offset access review note
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2073 review feedback on src/melee/vi/vi1202.c requested pulling the shown offset-style access into a struct, preferred, or using M2C_FIELD as a fallback.
source_update_candidates.reason: May guide future vi1202 cleanup around recovered struct layout and raw map/global offset accesses.

source_update_candidates.title: PR #2073 vi sequence matching touched multiple cross-module signatures
source_update_candidates.text: The PR body says vi intro/cutscene matching required gr/ and pl/ header updates. The diff also shows grbigblueroute.h, grcorneria.h, plbonuslib.h/c, and mncharsel.h signature cleanup.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path fact for future vi decomp work that appears to call into gr/pl/mn APIs with partially recovered signatures.

source_update_candidates.title: Check duplicate extern in ftKb_Init.static.h from PR #2073
source_update_candidates.text: The diff excerpt shows an added extern f32 ftKb_Init_804D9570 immediately adjacent to an existing identical extern declaration in src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_Init.static.h.
source_update_candidates.reason: This may be harmless or later cleaned up, but the loaded PR slice suggests duplicate declaration noise worth curator/source-owner review before recording any path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not record every bot-reported match as a permanent validation result without the final post-regression-fix report; the author comment says broken/regressed matches would be fixed.
Do not infer semantic meanings for address-style vi names; the PR largely retains un_/fn_/address-style naming.
Do not generalize the vi0102 helper expansion into a style rule; no reviewer or objdiff rationale for preferring expanded camera code over vi_RunCamera is included.
Do not promote it_804D6D40's f32* type as a broad data-ownership fact without checking current source/symbol metadata; the loaded diff only shows this PR's change.
